But I also lost my father after he passed away earlier this year. I decided to take what I have dubbed the Û÷Lea MicheleÛª approach to grief after being inspired by something she said in an interview about her own tragic loss - "at one point that there is an empowerment that comes with grief. At some point you find it. It's very hard, but you will find it.Û IÛªm not going to lie and say there hasnÛªt been some hard days and there has certainly been a lot of soul searching. What I have learned is there is always going to be highs and lows in life. It doesnÛªt need to be grief necessarily, but sadness, confusion or just road blocks that make you want to give up. DonÛªt give up. You never know who is watching how you handle your own trial as the inspiration on how to get through theirs. So donÛªt stop, donÛªt give in because they may never get to start if you do. You canÛªt control what comes to you, but you can control what comes through you. As with anything tough that comes your way - you can choose to fall, or you can choose to rise. All you can do is your best and hopefully make a positive impact through your choices & actions.
